transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling HarborPoint Health Line, this is Ana, your virtual care assistant. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, um, I've had a fever and this pounding headache since last night. It's not going away.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry you're feeling this way. Can you tell me your temperature, if you've checked it, and whether you have any stiff neck, rash, or confusion?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: It was 101.4 about an hour ago. No rash, no confusion, but my neck feels a little tight.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. Any sensitivity to light, vomiting, or trouble staying awake?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: A little light sensitivity, yeah. No vomiting though.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Given the fever, neck tightness, and light sensitivity together, I'd like you to be seen today rather than wait. This combination needs an in-person check to rule out something serious.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Oh. Should I go to the ER or urgent care?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Given the neck stiffness, please go to the nearest emergency room now rather than urgent care, just to be safe. Do you have someone who can drive you?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Yeah, my roommate can take me right now.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good. Bring a list of any medications you're on, and mention the fever, headache, and neck tightness together when you check in. I'll send a note to Dr. Ferris's office too.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, thank you, I'll head there now.
